title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo, from Scituate, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association in honor of her mother.
Cimo reflects on memories of her mother's kitchen, highlighting the emotional impact of Alzheimer's disease on family life.
The Marathon symbolizes Cimo's tribute to her mother's nurturing spirit, particularly through cooking and supporting family and friends.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins fall to Nashville 6-3, complicating trade deadline strategy with a 11-14-4 road record this season.
Boston holds second wild card spot with 34-22-5 record, but only one point ahead of Columbus.
General Manager Don Sweeney considers potential trades, favoring long-term benefits over acquiring older players.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge David Barron grants a stay of removal for Seamus Culleton, halting deportation pending review.
Culleton, an Irish national married to a U.S. citizen, has been detained in Texas for six months.
Culleton, arrested in Saugus, overstayed his visa in 2009 and faces unresolved 2008 drug-related charges in Ireland.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in a Foxborough house fire on Granite Street early Tuesday morning.
Firefighters from Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville responded to the blaze, which was reported around 3 a.m.
The cause of the fire, which caused serious damage to the home, is currently under investigation by officials.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree coverage increased by 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, raising the citywide coverage to 28.5%.
East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury saw the largest canopy gains; Hyde Park and Mattapan experienced losses.
The city's Urban Forest Plan aids in expanding the canopy, contributing to Boston's wider climate and environmental goals.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Kraft Group committed to covering World Cup security costs at Gillette Stadium, but Foxborough leaders remain dissatisfied.
Foxborough requires $7.8 million for security at seven World Cup games; the town is unconvinced by the current funding plan.
The Foxborough Select Board will meet March 17 to discuss granting the event license for the 2026 World Cup games.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway is closing after 10 years, with its final day on March 9, 2026.
The bar, popular with Northeastern students, cites rising insurance costs as a major factor for closing.
Owned by Chuck Hitchcock and partners, including Ken Casey, the bar generated 85% of its revenue from liquor sales.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is listed as questionable for the Celtics' game against the Mavericks after a 10-month injury absence.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla might impose a minutes restriction on Tatum to manage his recovery from Achilles surgery.
Tatum's return will affect team dynamics and player rotations, especially impacting young guards like Baylor Scheierman.

title: How did Jayson Tatum’s Achilles’ tendon recovery unfold? Recapping the Celtics star’s journey.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum's Achilles' tendon ruptured during the Eastern Conference semifinals, sidelining him for nearly 10 months.
Tatum underwent successful surgery on May 13, 2025, and attended media events indicating progress towards a full recovery.
The Celtics listed Tatum as questionable for a March 2026 game, signaling his expected return against the Mavericks.

title: Choosing which of my two cars to keep
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul suggests having a mechanic inspect both Honda Elements before deciding which one to keep.
The 2007 Honda Element has better safety features and fuel economy compared to the 2005 model.
John Paul recommends Battery Tender and C-Tek chargers for maintaining Hyundai 2023 Hybrid batteries.
